content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

June 7, 1984

Kirk Pessner
Nielsen, Hodgson, Parrinello
& Mueller
The Hartford Building
650 California Street, Suite 2650
San Francisco, CA 94108

RE: FPPC Advice No. A-84-156

Dear Kirk:

This is in reply to your letter dated June 5, 1984, and to
confirm our telephone conversation.

The filing requirements for the major donor committee are
correct as stated in your letter: (1) once the committee becomes
a "major donor" it is required to file campaign statements on the
"Standard Filing Schedule" as set out in Section A of the FPPC
"Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions" and is no
longer required to file campaign statements on the election
schedule for the April 10 election; (2) Government Code Section
84215(g) requires that a committee which begins filing its
statements only in a city or county, and subsequently is required
to file with the county or statewide must continue filing copies
in the city or county until the end of the year.

There is another possible filing obligation the committee
might incur, which is not mentioned in your letter and which we
did not discuss in our telephone conversation. If the committee
makes independent expenditures totaling $500 or more in
connection with a single ballot measure or a single candidate,
the committee must file a "Supplemental Independent Expenditure
Report" (Form 465). The Form 465 must be filed in the place and
at the time the committee would be required to file if it were
primarily formed to support or oppose the measure or candidate
supported or opposed by the independent expenditure. (Government
Code Section 84208). This supplemental report is in addition to
the committee's regular campaign disclosure statement (Form 461).

Please call me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

A 84-156

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SACRAMENTO                                THE HARTFORD BUILDING                JUN 5 1U 09 AM '84
1225 EIGHTH STREET, SUITE 550           650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814             SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752                 TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800
                                                                            FILE NUMBER

                                                                            5842.01


                                    June 5, 1984


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95807

                    Re:  Filing Deadlines

Dear Jeanne:

     Pursuant to our telephone conversation, I wish
to confirm what I understand your advice to have been.

     I described a situation in which an independent
expenditure committee which made both independent expenditures
and non-monetary contributions in one city with reference to
a measure to be voted on April 10, 1984, had filed the 12-day
pre-election filing covering the period January 1 through
March 24, 1984.  Subsequent to the close of that campaign
statement, the committee continued to make independent expendi-
tures and non-monetary contributions with reference to the city
measure being voted on April 10, 1984, and, therefore, has an
obligation to disclose, in that jurisdiction, contributions
made subsequent to the March 24, 1984, filing.

     The committee made additional monetary, non-monetary
and independent expenditures to measures being voted on the
June 5, 1984, ballot.  Pursuant to previous advice from you,
since the committee had reached both major donor and independent
expenditure thresholds, the committee was considered to be a
major donor committee and required to file on the six fixed
filing dates as outlined in Government Code Section 84200.
Therefore, the committee filed a campaign statement covering
the period March 25 through May 19, 1984.  Since all activity
by the filer was in one county, the filing was made with that
county, the county of domicile, and the city in which the
committee had previously filed.

     A 65-day post-campaign filing is due on June 14, 1984,
covering the period through June 7, 1984, for the April 10

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
June 5, 1984
page two


election. My question to you was whether or not this
committee was required to file on that deadline and, if so,
what period would the report cover. You advised me that
since the committee is now a major donor it must file on
the six fixed date filing deadlines only and not on any
other filing deadlines. You therefore indicated to me that
the next filing of the committee would be due July 31, 1984,
covering the period from May 20, 1984, through June 30, 1984,
and would be filed with the county in which the campaign
activity took place, the county of domicile, and the city
where campaign activity took place. The report would, of
course, contain all campaign information relating to the
election on April 10, 1984.

     If I have misstated our conversation, please do not
hesitate to contact me at your earliest convenience. If the
conversation is correctly stated here, please sign the attached
letter copy and return it to me in the enclosed envelope.
Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

                                   Kind regards,

                                   Kirk Alan Pessner
                                   Administrator
                                   Political Reports Unit

KAP:ddl
Enclosures

CONFIRMED BY:


_____________________________
JEANNE PRITCHARD

sections.conclusion: The filing requirements for the major donor committee are correct as stated in your letter: (1) once the committee becomes a "major donor" it is required to file campaign statements on the "Standard filing Schedule" as set out in Section A of the FPPC "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions" and is no longer required to file campaign statements on the election schedule for the April 10 election; (2) Government Code Section 84215(g) requires that a committee which begins filing its statements only in a city or county, and subsequently is required to file with the county or statewide must continue filing copies in the city or county until the end of the year. There is another possible filing obligation the committee might incur: If the committee makes independent expenditures totaling $500 or more in connection with a single ballot measure or a single candidate, the committee must file a "Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report" (Form 465).
sections.question_synthetic: When a committee becomes a major donor, must it file campaign statements on election-specific deadlines or only on the standard six fixed filing dates, and what period would subsequent reports cover?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Once a committee becomes a major donor, it must file on the six fixed filing dates under the standard schedule and is no longer required to file on election-specific deadlines. Additionally, if the committee makes independent expenditures of $500 or more for a single measure or candidate, it must file a supplemental independent expenditure report (Form 465) in addition to regular campaign disclosure statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of incoming request letter (June 5, 1984) followed by FPPC response letter (June 7, 1984). The response letter serves as the primary advice letter. Question is implicitly stated in the incoming request; conclusion is explicitly stated in the FPPC response.

QUESTION: When a committee becomes a major donor, must it file campaign statements on election-specific deadlines or only on the standard six fixed filing dates, and what period would subsequent reports cover?

CONCLUSION: The filing requirements for the major donor committee are correct as stated in your letter: (1) once the committee becomes a "major donor" it is required to file campaign statements on the "Standard filing Schedule" as set out in Section A of the FPPC "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions" and is no longer required to file campaign statements on the election schedule for the April 10 election; (2) Government Code Section 84215(g) requires that a committee which begins filing its statements only in a city or county, and subsequently is required to file with the county or statewide must continue filing copies in the city or county until the end of the year. There is another possible filing obligation the committee might incur: If the committee makes independent expenditures totaling $500 or more in connection with a single ballot measure or a single candidate, the committee must file a "Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report" (Form 465).

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-84-156 responds to an attorney's inquiry confirming that a major donor committee must file on standard six fixed filing dates rather than election-specific deadlines, and clarifies the additional filing requirement for independent expenditures exceeding $500 per measure or candidate.
